patches.summary: Surface the hand-authored magic-moment commands as named typed MCP tools (format-write-page, format-update-block, format-read-page, sync-database, sync-page, search-local, workflow-run); added a new `format read-page` command + internal/notionmd renderer (blocks -> extended markdown, the inverse of the writer).
patches.reason: The generated tools-manifest.json advertised only the 10 basic CRUD endpoint tools, so the writer/mirror/workflow magic-moments were invisible to MCP hosts reading the manifest, format-read-page did not exist, and the rest were reachable only under auto-generated cobra-tree mirror names. The new typed tools give them clean, documented names; the underlying CLI commands are marked mcp:hidden so the runtime mirror does not double-register them (same pattern as `search`).
patches.validated_outcome: go build/vet/test green; live MCP tools/list exposes all 7 named tools; format-read-page round-trips a written page back to extended markdown. sync stays MCP-reachable via sync-database (shell-out), preserving the sql/search-need-sync contract noted in cobratree/classify.go.

patches.summary: Added the public Notion REST endpoints the official Notion MCP exposes but this CLI's spec omitted, as typed MCP tools: databases (create/retrieve/update/query), comments (list/create), users (list/retrieve/me), pages-move (parent update), and pages-duplicate (compound copy of properties + full block tree). Views skipped (not in the public API).
patches.reason: The CLI was generated from a blocks-management spec (blocks/children/pages/search only), leaving a parity gap with the official Notion MCP for databases, comments, users, and page move/duplicate. Single endpoints call the API directly via makeAPIHandler (matching the existing endpoint tools); pages-duplicate is a compound handler because Notion has no single duplicate endpoint.
patches.validated_outcome: go build/vet/test green; tools-manifest.json and the context tool report 31 typed tools (10 endpoint + 3 framework + 7 magic-moment + 11 parity); mcp_tool_count bumped 10 -> 31.

deferred_to_upstream.feature: These REST endpoints (databases/comments/users) should ideally be generated from an expanded OpenAPI spec rather than hand-registered; a /printing-press-reprint against a fuller Notion spec would supersede this patch.
deferred_to_upstream.reason: Hand-registered tools are kept narrow and call the API directly; the generator should own endpoint typing/CLI-command generation when the spec covers these paths.
